strengths: The spec maintains a positive aggregate expectancy of +4.25 bps with a respectable 75.3% Win Rate over 89 trades.
The signal is highly predictive at very short horizons, achieving a Win Rate of 91.4% at the 1-tick horizon.
weaknesses: The addition of the `minute_of_session < 15` filter resulted in extremely inconsistent performance, with per-session Win Rates ranging from 0.0% (on 1 trade) to 100.0% (on 3 trades).

The filter significantly degraded performance compared to its parent spec (iter008_consensus_wide_trade_confirm), causing the Win Rate to drop from 96.2% to 75.3% and expectancy to fall from +6.83 bps to +4.25 bps.
The trade count was decimated, falling from 2,249 in the parent to just 89 in this spec, which is below the 100-trade threshold for robust statistical significance.
recommended_direction_reasoning: The addition of the `minute_of_session < 15` filter proved detrimental, introducing extreme performance instability as shown by per-session Win Rates ranging from 0.0% to 100.0%. This feature significantly worsened all key metrics compared to its high-performing parent. Dropping this unsuccessful regime filter is the necessary corrective action.
